# RAGWire — Full API Reference (Plain Text)

This file is the complete API reference for RAGWire in plain text format (no MkDocs syntax).
For the concise quick-reference, see llms.txt.
PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/ragwire/
GitHub: https://github.com/laxmimerit/ragwire

All public APIs are importable directly from `ragwire`:

```python
from ragwire import RAGWire, MarkItDownLoader, get_embedding, QdrantStore, ...
```

---

## Core API

### RAGWire

The main orchestrator. Handles the full pipeline from config loading to ingestion and retrieval.

```python
from ragwire import RAGWire
```

#### `RAGWire(config_path)`

Initialize the pipeline from a YAML config file.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `config_path` | `str` | Yes | Path to `config.yaml` |

Raises:
- `FileNotFoundError` — config file not found
- `ValueError` — missing required config keys (e.g. `llm.model`)

```python
rag = RAGWire("config.yaml")
```

---

#### `rag.ingest_documents(file_paths)`

Ingest a list of documents into the vector store. Skips files already ingested (SHA256 deduplication).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `file_paths` | `list[str]` | Yes | List of file paths to ingest |

Returns: `dict`

```python
{
    "total": 3,           # Total files submitted
    "processed": 2,       # Successfully ingested
    "skipped": 1,         # Already in vector store (duplicate)
    "failed": 0,          # Failed to load or process
    "chunks_created": 84, # Total chunks added to Qdrant
    "errors": []          # List of {"file": ..., "error": ...} dicts
}
```

```python
stats = rag.ingest_documents([
    "data/Apple_10k_2025.pdf",
    "data/Microsoft_10k_2025.pdf",
])
print(f"Processed: {stats['processed']}, Chunks: {stats['chunks_created']}")
```

A progress bar (tqdm) is shown automatically while ingestion runs.

---

#### `rag.ingest_directory(directory, recursive, extensions)`

Ingest all supported documents from a directory. Internally calls `ingest_documents()`.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `directory` | `str` | Yes | — | Path to the directory |
| `recursive` | `bool` | No | `False` | Search subdirectories |
| `extensions` | `list[str]` | No | loader config | File extensions to include |

Returns: `dict` — same stats dict as `ingest_documents()`

```python
stats = rag.ingest_directory("data/")
stats = rag.ingest_directory("data/", recursive=True)
stats = rag.ingest_directory("data/", extensions=[".pdf"])
```

---

#### `rag.retrieve(query, top_k, filters)`

Retrieve the most relevant chunks for a query.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `query` | `str` | Yes | — | Search query |
| `top_k` | `int` | No | config value | Number of results to return |
| `filters` | `dict` | No | `None` | Metadata filters |

Returns: `list[Document]`

Each Document has:
- `doc.page_content` — the chunk text (str)
- `doc.metadata` — dict with all metadata fields

Filter behaviour:
- If `filters` is passed → used as-is, no LLM call
- If `filters` is not passed and `auto_filter: true` in config → LLM extracts filters from the query
- If `filters` is not passed and `auto_filter: false` (default) → no filtering, pure semantic search

For agents: keep `auto_filter: false` and use `rag.extract_filters(query)` to give the agent control.

```python
# Explicit filters — LLM extraction skipped
results = rag.retrieve(
    "What is the net income?",
    top_k=5,
    filters={"company_name": "apple", "fiscal_year": 2025}
)

# OR logic — match chunks from any of these years
results = rag.retrieve("revenue trends", filters={"fiscal_year": [2023, 2024, 2025]})

for doc in results:
    print(doc.metadata.get("company_name"))
    print(doc.page_content[:300])
```

---

#### `rag.hybrid_search(query, k, filters)`

Perform hybrid search combining dense (semantic) and sparse (keyword) vectors.
Requires `use_sparse: true` in config and `pip install fastembed`.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `query` | `str` | Yes | — | Search query |
| `k` | `int` | No | `5` | Number of results |
| `filters` | `dict` | No | `None` | Metadata filters |

Returns: `list[Document]`

WARNING: If `fastembed` is not installed or `use_sparse: false`, this silently falls back to dense-only similarity search. No error is raised.

Use `retrieve()` when config has `search_type: "hybrid"` (identical results).
Use `hybrid_search()` only to force hybrid on a single call when config uses similarity/mmr.

```python
results = rag.hybrid_search(
    "Apple revenue fiscal 2025",
    k=5,
    filters={"company_name": "apple"}
)
```

---

#### `rag.extract_filters(query)`

Extract metadata filters from a natural language query without triggering retrieval.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `query` | `str` | Yes | Natural language query |

Returns: `dict` of extracted filters, or `None` if nothing was extracted.

This method always runs regardless of the `auto_filter` config setting. Use it to give agents explicit control over filter decisions.

```python
# Agent workflow — inspect and adjust before retrieval
filters = rag.extract_filters("Apple's 2025 annual report")
# → {"company_name": "apple", "fiscal_year": 2025} or None

if filters:
    results = rag.retrieve(query, filters=filters)
else:
    results = rag.retrieve(query)
```

---

#### `rag.get_filter_context(query, limit)`

Build a ready-made markdown block for an agent containing available metadata fields,
their stored values, filters extracted from the current query, and instructions.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `query` | `str` | Yes | — | Natural language query |
| `limit` | `int` | No | `50` | Max stored values to show per field |

Returns: `str` — formatted markdown block ready to inject into an agent prompt.

```python
filter_context = rag.get_filter_context("muscle building studies from 2023")
# Returns a markdown block with:
# - Available fields and their stored values
# - Extracted filters from the query
# - Instructions for the agent on how to use them
```

---

#### `rag.filter_fields`

Property. Returns the semantic/LLM-extracted metadata field names used for filtering.
Excludes system fields (file_hash, chunk_id, source, chunk_index, created_at).

```python
fields = rag.filter_fields
# Default: ['company_name', 'doc_type', 'fiscal_quarter', 'fiscal_year']
# Custom:  whatever fields are defined in your metadata.yaml

values = rag.get_field_values(fields)
# → {'company_name': ['apple', 'microsoft'], 'doc_type': ['10-k'], ...}
```

---

#### `rag.discover_metadata_fields()`

Return all metadata field names in the collection, including system fields.
Use for collection inspection/debugging. Use `rag.filter_fields` for filter prompts.

Returns: `list[str]`

```python
fields = rag.discover_metadata_fields()
# ['company_name', 'doc_type', 'fiscal_year', 'file_name', 'file_hash', 'chunk_id', ...]
```

---

#### `rag.get_field_values(fields, limit)`

Return unique values for one or more metadata fields (most common values first).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `fields` | `str \| list[str]` | Yes | — | Field name or list of field names |
| `limit` | `int` | No | `50` | Max unique values per field |

Returns:
- `list` if `fields` is a `str`
- `dict[str, list]` if `fields` is a `list`

```python
rag.get_field_values("company_name")
# → ['apple', 'microsoft', 'google']

rag.get_field_values(["company_name", "doc_type"])
# → {'company_name': ['apple', 'microsoft'], 'doc_type': ['10-k', '10-q']}

rag.get_field_values("file_name", limit=200)
# → ['Apple_10k_2025.pdf', 'Microsoft_10k_2025.pdf', ...]
```

---

#### `rag.extract_metadata(text)`

Extract structured metadata from text using the configured LLM.
Automatically grounds extraction against stored collection values.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `text` | `str` | Yes | Document text (first 10,000 chars used) |

Returns: `dict`

```python
metadata = rag.extract_metadata(open("report.txt").read())
# → {'company_name': 'apple', 'doc_type': '10-k', 'fiscal_quarter': None, 'fiscal_year': [2025]}
```

---

#### `rag.get_stats()`

Get statistics about the current collection.

Returns: `dict`

```python
{
    "collection_name": "financial_docs",
    "total_documents": 420,   # Total chunks in Qdrant
    "vector_size": 768,       # Embedding dimension
    "indexed": 420            # Number of indexed vectors
}
```

---

## Metadata Schema

Every retrieved `doc.metadata` is a dict with these keys:

```python
doc.metadata = {
    # LLM-extracted (financial defaults — customizable via metadata.yaml)
    "company_name":   "apple",          # str, always lowercased
    "doc_type":       "10-K",           # str: "10-K", "10-Q", "8-K", etc.
    "fiscal_quarter": "q4",             # str: "q1", "q2", "q3", "q4", or None
    "fiscal_year":    [2025],           # list[int] — NOT a plain int

    # File-level (auto-populated)
    "source":         "/data/Apple_10k_2025.pdf",
    "file_name":      "Apple_10k_2025.pdf",
    "file_type":      "pdf",
    "file_hash":      "a3f8...",        # SHA256 of file content

    # Chunk-level (auto-populated)
    "chunk_id":       "a3f8..._0",
    "chunk_hash":     "d7c2...",
    "chunk_index":    0,                # position within document
    "total_chunks":   42,              # total chunks from this file
    "created_at":     "2026-04-19T...", # ISO format UTC timestamp
}
```

CRITICAL: `fiscal_year` is stored as `list[int]` (e.g. `[2025]`).
When filtering, pass as `int`: `filters={"fiscal_year": 2025}`. RAGWire converts internally.

---

## Filter Syntax

```python
# Explicit (most common)
rag.retrieve(query, filters={"company_name": "apple"})
rag.retrieve(query, filters={"company_name": "apple", "fiscal_year": 2025})
rag.retrieve(query, filters={"fiscal_year": [2023, 2024, 2025]})  # OR within field

# auto_filter: true in config — LLM extracts filters from every query automatically
rag.retrieve(query)

# Agent-controlled
filters = rag.extract_filters(query)   # returns dict or None
results = rag.retrieve(query, filters=filters)
```

Filter rules:
- String values must be lowercase ("apple" not "Apple")
- fiscal_year takes int, not str
- List values = OR logic within field; multiple fields = AND logic across fields
- Use rag.get_field_values() to discover valid stored values

---

## Config Reference — llm, embeddings, vectorstore, retriever

### llm section

| Key | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `provider` | Yes | `ollama`, `openai`, `google`, `groq`, `anthropic` |
| `model` | Yes | Model name |
| `base_url` | Ollama only | Default: `http://localhost:11434` |
| `num_ctx` | Ollama only | Context window size |
| `api_key` | Some providers | API key or `${ENV_VAR}` |

Note: OpenAI reads `OPENAI_API_KEY` from environment automatically — no `api_key` field needed.

```yaml
# Ollama
llm:
  provider: "ollama"
  model: "qwen3.5:9b"
  base_url: "http://localhost:11434"
  num_ctx: 16384

# OpenAI
llm:
  provider: "openai"
  model: "gpt-4o-mini"

# Google Gemini
llm:
  provider: "google"
  model: "gemini-2.5-flash"
  api_key: "${GOOGLE_API_KEY}"

# Groq
llm:
  provider: "groq"
  model: "llama-3.3-70b-versatile"
  api_key: "${GROQ_API_KEY}"

# Anthropic
llm:
  provider: "anthropic"
  model: "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"
  api_key: "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}"
```

### embeddings section

| Key | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `provider` | Yes | `ollama`, `openai`, `google`, `huggingface`, `fastembed` |
| `model` | Most providers | Embedding model name |
| `model_name` | HuggingFace/FastEmbed | Uses `model_name` key (not `model`) |
| `base_url` | Ollama only | Ollama server URL |
| `api_key` | Google only | API key or `${ENV_VAR}` |

Default models: ollama=`nomic-embed-text`, openai=`text-embedding-3-small`,
google=`models/embedding-001`, huggingface=`sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2`,
fastembed=`BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5`

```yaml
# Ollama
embeddings:
  provider: "ollama"
  model: "qwen3-embedding:0.6b"

# OpenAI
embeddings:
  provider: "openai"
  model: "text-embedding-3-small"

# HuggingFace
embeddings:
  provider: "huggingface"
  model_name: "sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2"

# Google
embeddings:
  provider: "google"
  model: "models/gemini-embedding-001"
  api_key: "${GOOGLE_API_KEY}"

# FastEmbed
embeddings:
  provider: "fastembed"
  model_name: "BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5"
```

### vectorstore section

| Key | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `url` | Yes | — | Qdrant URL or local path |
| `collection_name` | Yes | — | Collection name |
| `api_key` | No | — | For Qdrant Cloud (use `${ENV_VAR}`) |
| `use_sparse` | No | `false` | Enable hybrid search (requires `pip install fastembed`) |
| `force_recreate` | No | `false` | WARNING: wipes the collection — use once then set back to false |

### retriever section

| Key | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `search_type` | No | `"similarity"` | `"similarity"`, `"mmr"`, or `"hybrid"` |
| `top_k` | No | `5` | Default number of results |
| `auto_filter` | No | `false` | Auto-extract filters from every query via LLM |

---

## Component API

### MarkItDownLoader

```python
from ragwire import MarkItDownLoader

loader = MarkItDownLoader()
result = loader.load("document.pdf")
# result: {success: bool, text_content: str, file_name: str, file_type: str, error: str|None}

results = loader.load_batch(["doc1.pdf", "doc2.pdf"])
results = loader.load_directory("data/", extensions=[".pdf", ".docx"], recursive=True)
```

### Text Splitters

```python
from ragwire import get_splitter, get_markdown_splitter, get_code_splitter

splitter = get_markdown_splitter(chunk_size=10000, chunk_overlap=2000)  # PDF/DOCX
splitter = get_splitter(chunk_size=5000, chunk_overlap=500)              # plain text
splitter = get_code_splitter(chunk_size=2000, chunk_overlap=200)         # source code

chunks = splitter.split_text(text)  # returns list[str]
```

### get_embedding

```python
from ragwire import get_embedding

embedding = get_embedding({"provider": "ollama", "model": "qwen3-embedding:0.6b", "base_url": "http://localhost:11434"})
embedding = get_embedding({"provider": "openai", "model": "text-embedding-3-small"})
embedding = get_embedding({"provider": "huggingface", "model_name": "sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2"})
embedding = get_embedding({"provider": "google", "model": "models/embedding-001"})
embedding = get_embedding({"provider": "fastembed", "model_name": "BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5"})

vector = embedding.embed_query("What is Apple's revenue?")
```

### MetadataExtractor

```python
from ragwire import MetadataExtractor, FinancialMetadata
from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama

llm = ChatOllama(model="qwen3.5:9b")

# Default financial schema (company_name, doc_type, fiscal_quarter, fiscal_year)
extractor = MetadataExtractor(llm)

# Custom Pydantic schema
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional

class MySchema(BaseModel):
    organization: Optional[str] = Field(None, description="Organization name in lowercase")
    doc_type: Optional[str] = Field(None, description="contract | policy | report")
    effective_year: Optional[int] = Field(None, description="Year the document is effective")

extractor = MetadataExtractor(llm, schema_model=MySchema)

# From YAML file
extractor = MetadataExtractor.from_yaml(llm, "metadata.yaml")

# Extract
meta = extractor.extract(document_text)                            # → dict
metas = extractor.extract_batch([text1, text2])                   # → list[dict]
meta = extractor.extract(text, stored_values={"company_name": ["apple"]})  # grounded
```

### Hashing

```python
from ragwire import sha256_text, sha256_file_from_path, sha256_chunk

sha256_file_from_path("document.pdf")  # → str (SHA256 of file)
sha256_text(content)                    # → str (SHA256 of text)
sha256_chunk(chunk_id, content)         # → str (SHA256 of id+content)
```

### QdrantStore (advanced)

```python
from ragwire import QdrantStore

store = QdrantStore(
    config={"url": "http://localhost:6333"},
    embedding=embedding,
    collection_name="my_docs",
)
store.set_collection("my_docs")
vectorstore = store.get_store(use_sparse=True)  # LangChain VectorStore

store.collection_exists()                          # → bool
store.file_hash_exists(file_hash)                  # → bool
store.get_metadata_keys()                          # → list[str]
store.get_field_values(["company_name"], limit=50) # → dict[str, list]
store.create_payload_indexes(["company_name"])      # create facet indexes
store.delete_collection()                          # WARNING: wipes data
```

### Retrieval Functions (advanced)

```python
from ragwire import get_retriever, hybrid_search, mmr_search

retriever = get_retriever(vectorstore, top_k=5, search_type="similarity")
results = retriever.invoke("query")

results = hybrid_search(vectorstore, "query", k=5, filters={"company_name": "apple"})

results = mmr_search(
    vectorstore, "query",
    k=5,              # results to return
    fetch_k=20,       # candidates before MMR selection
    lambda_mult=0.5,  # 0.0=max diverse, 1.0=max relevant
    filters=None,
)
```

### Logging

```python
from ragwire import setup_logging, setup_colored_logging, get_logger

logger = setup_logging(log_level="INFO", log_file="logs/rag.log", console_output=True)
logger = setup_colored_logging(log_level="DEBUG")  # colored console output
logger = get_logger(__name__)                       # child logger under ragwire namespace
```

---

## Complete Example: basic_usage.py

```python
"""
Example: Basic RAG Pipeline Usage
"""
from pathlib import Path
from ragwire import RAGWire, setup_logging

logger = setup_logging(log_level="INFO", console_output=True)

SCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).parent
DATA_DIR = SCRIPT_DIR / "data"
CONFIG_PATH = SCRIPT_DIR.parent / "config.yaml"

rag = RAGWire(str(CONFIG_PATH))

stats = rag.ingest_directory(str(DATA_DIR))
print(f"Processed: {stats['processed']}/{stats['total']}, Chunks: {stats['chunks_created']}")

# Basic retrieval
results = rag.retrieve("What is the total revenue?", top_k=3)
for doc in results:
    print(doc.metadata.get("company_name"), doc.metadata.get("fiscal_year"))
    print(doc.page_content[:300])

# Filter by company + year
results = rag.retrieve(
    "net income",
    top_k=3,
    filters={"company_name": "apple", "fiscal_year": 2025},
)

# Hybrid search
results = rag.hybrid_search("apple revenue fiscal 2025", k=3)

# Inspect metadata on first chunk
results = rag.retrieve("revenue", top_k=1)
for key, value in results[0].metadata.items():
    print(f"  {key}: {value}")
```

---

## Complete Example: rag_agent.py (LangChain Agent)

```python
from typing import Optional
from langchain.agents import create_agent
from langchain.tools import tool
from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage
from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import InMemorySaver
from ragwire import RAGWire

rag = RAGWire("config.yaml")
rag.ingest_directory("data/")

@tool
def get_filter_context(query: str) -> str:
    """Get available metadata fields and filter suggestions for this query.
    Call before search_documents when the query involves specific metadata
    (company, year, document type). Skip for purely semantic queries."""
    return rag.get_filter_context(query)

@tool
def search_documents(query: str, filters: Optional[dict] = None) -> str:
    """Search the document knowledge base.
    Args:
        query: The search query
        filters: Optional metadata filters from get_filter_context. Pass {} to search without filtering."""
    results = rag.retrieve(query, top_k=5, filters=filters)
    if not results:
        return "No relevant documents found."
    chunks = []
    for doc in results:
        source = doc.metadata.get("file_name", "unknown")
        meta = {k: v for k, v in doc.metadata.items() if k != "file_name" and v not in (None, "", [])}
        chunks.append(f"[{source} | {meta}]\n{doc.page_content}")
    return "\n\n---\n\n".join(chunks)

model = ChatOllama(model="qwen3.5:9b", base_url="http://localhost:11434")
agent = create_agent(
    model=model,
    tools=[get_filter_context, search_documents],
    system_prompt=(
        "Always use search_documents to retrieve information. "
        "Use get_filter_context before search_documents when the query involves "
        "specific metadata (company, year, document type). "
        "Never answer from general knowledge. Always cite the source."
    ),
    checkpointer=InMemorySaver(),
)

config = {"configurable": {"thread_id": "demo"}}

while True:
    question = input("You: ").strip()
    if question.lower() in ("quit", "exit", "q"):
        break
    response = agent.invoke({"messages": [HumanMessage(question)]}, config=config)
    print(f"\nAgent: {response['messages'][-1].content}\n")
```

**Agent reasoning flow:**
1. Query arrives
2. Call `get_filter_context` if query mentions specific company/year/type
3. Use returned context to decide `filters` dict
4. Call `search_documents(query, filters=filters)`
5. Answer from retrieved chunks

---

## config.example.yaml

```yaml
# RAGWire Configuration Example
# Copy this file to config.yaml and edit for your environment.

loader:
  extensions: [".pdf", ".docx", ".xlsx", ".pptx", ".txt", ".md"]

splitter:
  chunk_size: 10000
  chunk_overlap: 2000
  strategy: "markdown"   # "markdown" | "recursive"

embeddings:
  # Ollama (local, no API key)
  provider: "ollama"
  model: "qwen3-embedding:0.6b"
  base_url: "http://localhost:11434"

  # OpenAI: provider: "openai" / model: "text-embedding-3-small"
  # HuggingFace: provider: "huggingface" / model_name: "sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2"
  # Google: provider: "google" / model: "models/embedding-001"

llm:
  provider: "ollama"
  model: "qwen3.5:9b"
  base_url: "http://localhost:11434"
  num_ctx: 16384

  # OpenAI: provider: "openai" / model: "gpt-4o-mini"
  # Google: provider: "google" / model: "gemini-2.5-flash" / api_key: "${GOOGLE_API_KEY}"
  # Groq: provider: "groq" / model: "llama-3.3-70b-versatile" / api_key: "${GROQ_API_KEY}"
  # Anthropic: provider: "anthropic" / model: "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001" / api_key: "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}"

# metadata:
#   config_file: "metadata.yaml"   # Custom metadata fields (see metadata.example.yaml)

vectorstore:
  url: "http://localhost:6333"
  # api_key: "${QDRANT_API_KEY}"   # For Qdrant Cloud
  collection_name: "financial_docs"
  use_sparse: true       # Enables hybrid search. Requires: pip install fastembed
  force_recreate: false  # WARNING: true WIPES the collection.

retriever:
  search_type: "hybrid"   # "similarity" | "mmr" | "hybrid"
  top_k: 5
  auto_filter: false       # Keep false for agents; use rag.extract_filters() instead.

logging:
  level: "INFO"
  console_output: true
  colored: true
  # log_file: "logs/rag.log"
```

---

## metadata.example.yaml

```yaml
# RAGWire Custom Metadata Configuration
# Reference from config.yaml: metadata: { config_file: "metadata.yaml" }

fields:
  - name: organization
    description: "Organization name in lowercase"

  - name: doc_type
    description: "Type of document"
    values: ["contract", "policy", "report", "memo", "other"]

  - name: effective_year
    description: "Year the document is effective, as integer or null"

  - name: jurisdiction
    description: "Country or region the document applies to, or null"

# Optional: override auto-generated prompt entirely (must include {content} placeholder)
# prompt_template: |
#   Extract metadata from the document. Return ONLY valid JSON:
#   {
#     "organization": "organization name in lowercase",
#     "doc_type": "contract|policy|report|memo|other",
#     "effective_year": year as integer or null,
#     "jurisdiction": "country or region or null"
#   }
#   Document Text:
#   {content}
#   Extracted Metadata (JSON only):
```
